title: Peloton Q4 2024 Earnings Report
text: Peloton Q4 Fiscal 2024 Earnings Results. Sales Growth: Sales grew by 0.2% during its fiscal fourth quarter, marking the first time Peloton posted year-over-year revenue growth since its 2021 holiday quarter. This inflection point was significant for a company that had been contracting for multiple consecutive quarters. Profitability Improvement: Loss per share was 8 cents versus 17 cents expected, beating analyst expectations. Revenue was $644 million versus $631 million expected, representing a beat on both top and bottom line metrics. Balance Sheet: Peloton narrowed its quarterly losses to $30.5 million from $241.1 million in the year-ago period, a significant improvement. Subscription Segment: Subscription revenue rose by 2.3%, and the segment's gross margin increased by 1 percentage point, indicating improving unit economics. Secondary Market Growth: Subscription revenue from hardware purchased on the secondary market grew 16% year over year, representing a new revenue stream. Strategic Shift: The Bike and Tread maker has been working to improve its balance sheet and looks to be more focused on profitability than growth, a significant strategic shift from the company's historical growth-at-all-costs approach.
